title: Malty and tender Welsh Apricot Jam Ploughman's Tart
description: A hearty, comforting tart featuring malty pastry, tender apricots, and a hint of sweetness, perfect for a lazy Sunday afternoon in Wales.
instructions: Preheat oven to 3757c (190f) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Roll out the pastry dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch, then transfer it to the prepared baking sheet.
Arrange the fresh apricots on one half of the pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Fold the other half of the pastry over the apricots, pressing the edges to seal.
Dust the pastry with flour and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake the tart for 25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the apricots are tender.
While the tart is baking, mix the apricot jam and honey in a small bowl until well combined.
Remove the tart from the oven and brush the jam mixture evenly over the apricots.
Return the tart to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes, or until the glaze is set.
Allow the tart to cool on a wire rack for at least 10 minutes before serving.
